channel.title: Posts on Walking cross the paths
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Walking cross the paths

item.title: User Research I
item.description:
Speaker: Bryan Guo

User research is important!

The top 1 reason why start-ups fail is no market need.

Advices

Talk to real users in the market to test your assumption about their need.

item.title: Guest Tarran
item.description:
Speaker: Ben Guo
Key takeaways:

  1. Think globally: market, think big
  2. Surround Yourself with Teachers & Friends
  3. Move Fast and Break things.
  4. Be the best.
  5. For hardware, try lots of versions concurrently in one iteration and pick one best

item.title: Assets Mapping
item.description:
1. How to approach a problem? How to identify opportunity or pain point?
  • Think divergently & reframe. e.g., watch vs portable time device
  • Levels of solution: incremental or disruptive or systemic innovation
  1. Key design concepts
    1. Think holisticly
    2. Make abstract ideas tangible
    3. Approach problem in a human-center manner
    4. Design collaboratively
    5. Design for simplicity
  2. Community Assets Map: Financial capital, cultural capital, social capital, etc.
  3. Map the assets and problems/opportunities

item.title: Sources
item.description:
• CSDI (Common Spatial Data Infrastructure): https://portal.csdi.gov.hk/csdi-webpage/
    information infrastructure to share spatial data of Hong Kong, supporting various smart city applications

item.title: Design Thinking I
item.description:
By Prof. Joseph Chan

Advice for maximizing InboX value

  • Reach out for resources proactively: Work space, personnel, industry connection
  • Be open-minded about
    • Project topic: Embrace changes from time to time
    • Team members: No competition in the camp. 7/10 entered the science park incumbation programme. Goal is to develop a incumbation-ready level.
  • Think about product value - to customers, to investors
  • Prototype quickly and iteratively
  • Empathy: Understand what customers real need
    • Positive need prompts consumption more than negative need. e.g., swimming
    • Product hits
  • Think about what is your core tech.

Two diamond diagram for design

Pitch Deck Structure

A sample is like this:
